summary: Excellent matrix recording of a high‑energy 1981 show with scorching Shakedown and Playin' in the Band jams
review: The matrix mix is praised as warm, clear and full‑bodied, delivering an almost studio‑like presence that lets every instrument shine. Musically the band is described as tightly together, launching a fiery set that kicks off with a superb Shakedown and builds through standout renditions of Playin' in the Band, Let It Grow, Morning Dew and a ripping Bertha, only letting Not Fade Away slip a bit.

ai_rating.rationale: All but one reviewer gave five stars; the show is repeatedly called “smoking”, “awesome” and “one of the best ever,” while the matrix sound quality is described as fantastic with no complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note a warm, crisp mix with great depth; board/audience blend is praised for clarity and lack of audible glitches or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The band opens with a tight Shakedown that sets an upbeat vibe, moves through spacey jams (Playin' in the Band) and segues smoothly into classic favorites; energy stays high throughout both sets with only the NFA transition noted as less cohesive.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is repeatedly called “liquid,” “on fire,” and “ripping”; many reviewers say he leads the jams with superb creativity.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s rhythm work and vocal interplay are highlighted, especially his smooth transition into "CC Rider" and his chemistry with Jerry during Playin'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) gets credit for boosting DEMI and providing spacey textures that enhance the overall vibe.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are described as “very well down” and “spacey,” adding to the cosmic feel of the performance.
